title: Twitter @tomaarsen
summary:
Very cool work on sparse neural embedding models: think BM25 but with a semantic component. 

Always glad to see novelty in sparse embeddings - their lexical focus and interpretability are very useful for search.

title: Twitter @tomaarsen
summary:
Very cool work for training a dense embedding model -> sparse embedding model that accepts variable dimensionalities (akin to Matryoshka in dense models)

I'm quite interested in adding support for training and inference of sparse embedding models in Sentence Transformers.

title: Twitter @yifeiwang77
summary:
No More Tears with Matryoshka🪆 & Let's Embrace Sparsity 🚀🚀

With sparse autoencoders + sparse contrastive learning, we can compress SOTA text/image/multimodal embedding models from 2k/4k dimensions to 16 active dimensions with: ~100x faster at large-scale retrieval, minimal https://t.co/KAwo7GFpdT

title: Twitter @drexalt
summary: there's gotta be a way to feed sparse encoder predictions to an llm to generate synthetic hard negatives. "here are the important tokens, give me hard negatives with these"
